evals.name: docs-vs-config-port-drift
evals.prompt: Run context-gc on this repo. README.md says the API runs on port 8000, but docker-compose.yml maps the service to 8080. Diagnose the drift and propose a fix, but do not edit yet.

files.README.md:
# Service

Start the API at http://localhost:8000.

evals.expected_output: A MARK-only entropy report that flags README.md as stale/contradictory against docker-compose.yml, proposes reconciling to 8080, and mentions SOURCES.md as a write barrier.
assertions: States that Phase 1/MARK is read-only and does not edit files
Flags the port mismatch as 🔴 CONTRADICTION or 🟠 STALE
Identifies docker-compose.yml as the likely root for the live port
Proposes updating README.md to 8080
Mentions SOURCES.md or authority map/write barrier

evals.name: local-prod-config-version-drift
evals.prompt: Our local config.yaml has _config_version: 27 while the server migrated config has _config_version: 29. Treat this as context/config drift and produce a sweep plan.
evals.expected_output: A configuration drift report and gated sweep plan that treats the server migrated snapshot as the likely root only with justification or asks for confirmation before overwriting local config.
assertions: Treats this as configuration drift rather than only documentation cleanup
States or asks which config is authoritative/root
Produces a sweep plan gated by confirmation
Does not directly overwrite config.yaml without approval
Proposes recording root-to-copy relationship in SOURCES.md

evals.name: agent-context-rot-dead-skill
evals.prompt: GC this agent context. SOUL.md still tells the agent to use a deleted skill called old-scraper, while skills/new-scraper/SKILL.md exists. There are also three overlapping rules about scraping limits.

files.SOUL.md:
Use old-scraper for all collection.
Limit scraping to 10 req/s.
Never exceed 5 req/s.
Scraping should be conservative.

evals.expected_output: An agent-context-rot entropy report that marks old-scraper as orphan/dead reference and the scraping limit rules as contradiction/duplication, then proposes compacting to one policy after confirmation and recording the agent policy root in SOURCES.md.
assertions: Recognizes this as AI-agent context rot
Flags old-scraper as an orphan/dead reference
Flags overlapping scraping rules as duplication or contradiction
Proposes compacting to one authoritative scraping policy
Mentions SOURCES.md or authority map/write barrier
Requires user confirmation before editing SOUL.md

evals.name: knowledge-base-duplication-compaction
evals.prompt: Our knowledge base has the same deployment instructions copied in README.md, docs/deploy.md, and wiki/export.md. They currently agree, but I want to prevent future drift. Run context-gc.

files.README.md: Deploy: run ./deploy.sh and then restart service.
files.docs/deploy.md: Deploy: run ./deploy.sh and then restart service.
files.wiki/export.md: Deploy: run ./deploy.sh and then restart service.

evals.expected_output: A duplication/compaction report that treats repeated deployment instructions as a drift factory, asks or chooses a canonical root, and proposes replacing duplicates with pointers plus SOURCES.md after confirmation.
assertions: Flags duplication as 🟡 duplicate or drift factory even though copies agree
Chooses or asks for a canonical root
Proposes replacing duplicate restatements with pointers
Requires user confirmation before editing duplicate docs
Mentions SOURCES.md/write barrier
Explains why duplication creates future contradictions

evals.name: sdd-drift-after-requirement-change
evals.prompt: Run context-gc on this SDD and implementation. docs/sdd.md says the current auth flow is email/password, but src/auth_flow.py and tests/test_auth_flow.py use OAuth device flow after a requirement change. Diagnose the drift and propose a sweep plan, but do not edit yet.

files.docs/sdd.md:
# Auth SDD

## Current behavior

Users authenticate with an email and password form.

evals.expected_output: An SDD drift report that flags SPEC_DRIFT/UNKNOWN_ROOT, cites docs/sdd.md against src/auth_flow.py and tests, asks whether code/tests or the SDD is authoritative, requires confirmation before editing, and proposes recording the confirmed root in SOURCES.md.
assertions: Recognizes this as SDD/spec drift rather than generic stale docs
Flags the mismatch as SPEC_DRIFT with UNKNOWN_ROOT until authority is confirmed
Cites both docs/sdd.md and code/tests evidence
Asks whether to update docs to code or code to spec
Requires user confirmation before editing
Mentions SOURCES.md or authority map/write barrier

evals.name: historical-adr-preserve
evals.prompt: Run context-gc on this auth documentation. docs/adr-003-password-login.md records that we chose password login in 2024, while docs/sdd.md and src/auth_flow.py now describe OAuth device flow. Diagnose the mismatch, but do not edit yet.

files.docs/adr-003-password-login.md:
# ADR-003: Password login

Status: Superseded

In 2024, we chose email/password login because OAuth device flow was not available.
files.docs/sdd.md:
# Auth SDD

## Current behavior

Users authenticate with OAuth device flow.

evals.expected_output: A MARK-only entropy report that classifies the ADR as HISTORICAL rather than stale SDD drift, preserves the historical decision, optionally proposes a superseded/current pointer, and records the current-vs-historical relationship in SOURCES.md without rewriting the ADR to current behavior.
assertions: Classifies docs/adr-003-password-login.md as HISTORICAL, not garbage to delete
Does not rewrite the 2024 password-login decision into OAuth device flow
Preserves the ADR as historical evidence and may add a pointer to the current SDD
Identifies docs/sdd.md or src/auth_flow.py as the current behavior root
Mentions SOURCES.md or authority map/write barrier
Requires confirmation before any edit

evals.name: intentional-config-fork
evals.prompt: Run context-gc on these environment configs. config.local.yaml uses localhost with debug enabled, while config.prod.yaml uses the production API with debug disabled. The local file is intentionally different for development. Diagnose without forcing convergence.

files.docs/config.md: Local development uses localhost and debug mode. Production uses https://api.example.com with debug disabled.

evals.expected_output: An entropy report that classifies the local/prod difference as FORK, explains it is intentional environment divergence rather than drift, avoids reconciling local config to production, and proposes documenting the exception/scope in SOURCES.md.
assertions: Classifies the local/prod config difference as FORK rather than DRIFTED garbage
Explains that intentional environment divergence should not be force-converged
Does not propose overwriting config.local.yaml with production values
Documents the exception or scope in SOURCES.md
Requires confirmation before changing config docs or authority maps

evals.name: agent-semantic-instruction-conflict
evals.prompt: Run context-gc on this agent context. CLAUDE.md says scraping must stay under 5 req/s, SOUL.md says collection may use 10 req/s, and memory/scraping-policy.md says prefer conservative scraping. Diagnose agent drift without editing.

files.SOUL.md: Use crawler-skill for collection. Limit scraping to 10 req/s when needed.
files.memory/scraping-policy.md: Prefer conservative scraping; official APIs first.

evals.expected_output: An agent drift entropy report that flags semantic-instruction-cluster / CONFUSION or CONTRADICTION across CLAUDE.md, SOUL.md, and memory, asks which policy is authoritative, proposes compacting to one anchor, and records the agent policy root in SOURCES.md after confirmation.
assertions: Recognizes semantic near-duplicate agent instructions, not only exact duplicate lines
Flags the 5 req/s versus 10 req/s mismatch as CONFUSION or CONTRADICTION
Asks which agent policy is authoritative before sweeping
Proposes compacting to one anchor instruction
Mentions SOURCES.md or authority map/write barrier

evals.name: agent-memory-leak-compaction
evals.prompt: Run context-gc on this agent memory directory. It has dated variants of the same user preference/status fact. Diagnose memory leak and propose a gated compaction plan.

files.memory/user-preference-2026-02.md: User prefers concise answers with examples.
files.memory/user-preference-2026-03.md: User prefers concise answers with examples and no long preambles.

evals.expected_output: An agent memory-leak / BLOAT report that detects append-only dated variants, proposes condensing them into one durable current memory while preserving useful history, requires confirmation, and records memory authority in SOURCES.md.
assertions: Flags append-only memory growth as BLOAT or memory leak
Identifies repeated dated variants of the same memory fact
Proposes condensing to one durable current fact
Does not delete memory automatically
Mentions SOURCES.md or authority map/write barrier

evals.name: agent-skill-bloat-budget
evals.prompt: Run context-gc on this agent skill set. Several skills overlap and one SKILL.md is very large. Produce an agent context budget report and sweep plan, but do not edit yet.

files.skills/search/SKILL.md:
---
name: search
---
Use this skill for web search and source review.
files.skills/research/SKILL.md:
---
name: research
---
Use this skill for web search, source review, and synthesis.

evals.expected_output: An agent skill-bloat / DISTRACTION report that treats overlapping or oversized skills as context budget pressure, recommends review/freezing/splitting only after confirmation, and records active skill roots in SOURCES.md.
assertions: Recognizes skill/tool bloat as agent context drift or distraction
Includes a context budget or size-pressure discussion
Does not delete or archive skills without confirmation
Proposes freezing, splitting, or consolidating overlapping skills
Mentions SOURCES.md or authority map/write barrier

evals.name: agent-session-context-rot
evals.prompt: Run context-gc on this exported transcript. Early turns approve Plan A, later turns cancel it and switch to Plan B, while old TODOs and long tool output remain. Diagnose session context rot without compacting automatically.

files.transcript.md:
User: Plan A is approved.
Assistant: TODO implement Plan A.
Tool output:
```
line
line
line
```
User: Cancel Plan A; instead use Plan B.
Assistant: Decision: Plan B supersedes Plan A.

evals.expected_output: A session entropy report that flags stale-plan-signal, orphaned-session-task, repeated/session budget or tool-output bloat, proposes transcript compaction into durable decisions plus evidence pointers, states compaction is gated/read-only until confirmation, and records session-context handling in SOURCES.md.
assertions: Recognizes a transcript/session as a context heap
Flags Plan A as superseded by Plan B rather than preserving both as current
Flags stale TODO or tool-output bloat
Proposes transcript compaction with evidence pointers
Does not silently delete session evidence

evals.name: agent-tone-behavior-drift
evals.prompt: Run context-gc on this agent behavior context. CLAUDE.md says be concise and direct, while SOUL.md says be warm and explain step by step in detail. Diagnose tone/behavior drift without editing.

files.CLAUDE.md: Be concise and direct. Avoid long explanations.
files.SOUL.md: Be warm, friendly, and explain every answer step-by-step in detailed prose.

evals.expected_output: An agent tone/behavior drift report that flags CLASH or UNKNOWN_ROOT between concise/direct and warm/detailed instructions, asks which behavior policy is the root, proposes compacting exceptions, records the behavior root in SOURCES.md, and requires confirmation before editing.
assertions: Recognizes tone/behavior drift as agent context rot
Flags concise/direct versus warm/detailed as CLASH or UNKNOWN_ROOT
Asks which behavior policy wins
Proposes one anchor behavior rule or scoped FORK exceptions
Requires confirmation before editing

evals.name: minor-gc-safe-port-autofix
evals.prompt: Run preventive Minor GC on this dirty README. SOURCES.md declares docker-compose.yml as the root for the local API port and allows scalar-sync. Apply only safe pre-authorized fixes.

files.README.md:
# Service

Start the API at http://localhost:8000.
files.SOURCES.md:
### `service-api-port`

- **Root:** `docker-compose.yml`
- **Copies:**
  - `README.md` — documents local API URL
- **Auto-fix:** `scalar-sync`
- **Root extract:** `"(\d+):\1"`
- **Copy replace:** `localhost:(\d+)`
- **Protected:** `false`
- **Status:** `SYNCED`

evals.expected_output: A Minor GC report with AUTO_FIXED for README.md, changing localhost:8000 to localhost:8080 using the pre-authorized scalar-sync contract, and no edits outside declared safe copies.
assertions: Recognizes Minor GC as preventive drift control
Applies scalar-sync only because SOURCES.md declares Auto-fix
Reports AUTO_FIXED with 8000 to 8080
Does not require human approval for this pre-authorized low-risk fix
Does not edit protected or undeclared files

evals.name: minor-gc-protected-agent-conflict
evals.prompt: Run preventive Minor GC on this agent context. CLAUDE.md and SOUL.md disagree on scraping limits, but both are protected. Do not auto-edit them.

files.SOUL.md: Limit scraping to 10 req/s when needed.
files.SOURCES.md:
### `agent-policy`

- **Root:** `CLAUDE.md`
- **Copies:**
  - `SOUL.md` — reviewed manually
- **Auto-fix:** `scalar-sync`
- **Root extract:** `(\d+) req/s`
- **Copy replace:** `(\d+) req/s`
- **Protected:** `true`
- **Status:** `UNKNOWN_ROOT`

evals.expected_output: A Minor GC report that skips the agent policy with PROTECTED_SKIP or UNKNOWN_ROOT_SKIP, writes a compact report for review, records the protected policy in SOURCES.md, requires confirmation before any protected edit, and does not edit CLAUDE.md or SOUL.md.
assertions: Treats agent root files as protected
Skips auto-fix for UNKNOWN_ROOT or protected policy
Reports PROTECTED_SKIP or UNKNOWN_ROOT_SKIP
Does not change CLAUDE.md or SOUL.md
Minimizes human involvement by producing a compact escalation report

evals.name: minor-gc-historical-skip
evals.prompt: Run preventive Minor GC where an ADR still mentions password login but current code uses OAuth. SOURCES.md marks the ADR domain HISTORICAL. Do not auto-edit history.
evals.expected_output: A Minor GC report with HISTORICAL_SKIP for the ADR domain, preserving history, recording the historical exception in SOURCES.md, requiring confirmation before any historical edit, and avoiding any automatic rewrite to OAuth.
assertions: Recognizes HISTORICAL as report-only in Minor GC
Reports HISTORICAL_SKIP
Does not rewrite the ADR to current behavior
Preserves historical evidence
Requires review for any historical compaction

evals.name: minor-gc-fork-skip
evals.prompt: Run preventive Minor GC on local/prod config differences that SOURCES.md marks as FORK. Do not force convergence.
evals.expected_output: A Minor GC report with FORK_SKIP for intentional local/prod divergence, recording the fork exception in SOURCES.md, requiring confirmation before any fork edit, and avoiding any automatic convergence of config.local.yaml to production.
assertions: Recognizes FORK as intentional divergence
Reports FORK_SKIP
Does not overwrite local config with production values
Documents the skip in the Minor GC report
Keeps human involvement limited to review if desired

evals.name: memory-drift-profile-conflict
evals.prompt: Run context-gc on this agent memory. memory/profile.md says the user enjoys verbose, detailed answers, while memory/user-preference.md says concise. Diagnose memory/profile drift without editing.

files.memory/profile.md:
---
memory_type: profile
subject: user-preference
---
User profile: enjoys verbose, detailed walkthroughs.

evals.expected_output: A memory-drift report that flags profile-drift / memory-conflict between profile and preference memory as UNKNOWN_ROOT, asks which is current, does not silently overwrite either memory, and records the reconciled memory authority in SOURCES.md after confirmation.
assertions: Recognizes profile vs preference memory drift
Flags concise vs verbose as memory-conflict or profile-drift
Does not silently overwrite profile or preference memory
Asks which memory layer is current before any edit
Mentions SOURCES.md or authority map/write barrier

evals.name: memory-condense-safe-current-summary
evals.prompt: Run preventive Minor GC on this agent memory. SOURCES.md declares a memory-condense contract for dated user-preference variants with a Memory target. Apply only safe pre-authorized memory cleanup.

files.memory/user-preference-2026-02.md: User prefers concise answers with short examples.
files.memory/user-preference-2026-03.md: User prefers concise answers with short examples and no long preambles.
files.SOURCES.md:
### `user-verbosity-preference`

- **Root:** `memory/user-preference-2026-03.md`
- **Copies:**
  - `memory/user-preference-2026-01.md` — superseded candidate
  - `memory/user-preference-2026-02.md` — superseded candidate
- **Auto-fix:** `memory-condense`
- **Memory subject:** `user-preference:verbosity`
- **Memory target:** `memory/current/user-preference.md`
- **Status:** `SYNCED`

evals.expected_output: A memory GC result that writes memory/current/user-preference.md from the declared Root, keeps every dated variant as evidence, deletes nothing, and runs only because the memory-condense contract and config opt-in are present.
assertions: Writes a single current memory only from the declared memory-condense contract
Uses the declared Root as the canonical current memory
Lists the original dated memories as evidence
Does not delete the original memory files
Runs only with pre-authorized contract approval and config opt-in
Mentions SOURCES.md or authority map/write barrier

evals.name: memory-condense-ambiguous-skip
evals.prompt: Run preventive Minor GC on a memory-condense contract whose sources conflict: one says concise, one says verbose, equally recent. Do not auto-resolve.
evals.expected_output: A memory GC result that reports CONFLICT_NEEDS_REVIEW for the conflicting memories, writes no current memory automatically, and escalates the conflict for review while recording it in SOURCES.md.
assertions: Detects the concise vs verbose conflict within the contract
Reports CONFLICT_NEEDS_REVIEW
Does not auto-write a current memory
Escalates the ambiguous conflict for review
Requires human confirmation to resolve the conflicting memory
Mentions SOURCES.md or authority map/write barrier

evals.name: midterm-memory-expired
evals.prompt: Run context-gc on this mid-term memory. memory/mid-term/plan-a.md tracks a plan that has since been cancelled. Diagnose stale mid-term memory without editing.

files.memory/mid-term/plan-a.md:
---
memory_type: mid-term
subject: plan-a
---
Active plan: implement Plan A. Status: cancelled in favor of Plan B.

evals.expected_output: A memory-drift report that flags the mid-term Plan A memory as midterm-expired, suggests archiving or marking it superseded, preserves it as evidence, and records the active plan authority in SOURCES.md after confirmation.
assertions: Recognizes mid-term memory as a distinct memory layer
Flags the cancelled Plan A memory as midterm-expired or stale
Suggests archive or status: superseded rather than silent deletion
Preserves the mid-term memory as evidence
Mentions SOURCES.md or authority map/write barrier

evals.name: guided-setup-authorization
evals.prompt: Set up context-gc to manage drift in this repo. Walk me through guided setup, but do not edit my files until I confirm each choice.

files.README.md:
# Service

Start the API at http://localhost:8000.

evals.expected_output: A guided setup that runs init --guided, reads setup-draft.json, asks one AskUserQuestion per ambiguous root, asks the single authorization question about background auto-fix, then writes confirmed SOURCES.md and config with safe defaults (auto-MARK on, apply_safe only if authorized). Requires confirmation before each write.
assertions: Runs init with the guided flag and reads setup-draft.json
Asks the user which source is authoritative for ambiguous roots
Asks one authorization question before enabling background auto-fix
Leaves sensitive agent/memory fixes review-only regardless of the answer
Records confirmed roots in SOURCES.md
Requires confirmation before writing

evals.name: review-resolves-memory-conflict
evals.prompt: Review the waiting drift decisions. The review queue has a memory verbosity conflict between profile (verbose) and preference (concise). Help me resolve it as a quick choice, and confirm before applying.

files.memory/profile.md: User profile: prefers verbose, detailed explanations.
files..context-gc/review-queue.json: {"open": 1, "items": [{"id": "memory-conflict-1", "kind": "memory-conflict", "summary": "memory says concise; profile says verbose", "evidence": ["memory/preference.md", "memory/profile.md"], "options": [{"label": "Keep concise", "action": {"op": "set_current_memory", "from": "memory/preference.md"}}, {"label": "Keep verbose", "action": {"op": "set_current_memory", "from": "memory/profile.md"}}, {"label": "Both, scope by context", "action": {"op": "mark_fork"}}], "recommend": -1, "status": "open"}]}

evals.expected_output: A review flow that reads review-queue.json, presents the memory conflict as one AskUserQuestion with the three labeled options, performs the chosen declarative action after confirmation, records the resolved memory authority in SOURCES.md, and removes the item from the queue.
assertions: Reads the review queue and presents one choice per open item
Surfaces the memory conflict as a labeled multiple-choice decision
Performs the chosen option's declarative action only after confirmation
Records the resolved authority in SOURCES.md
Marks the queue item resolved

evals.name: review-preserves-evidence
evals.prompt: Resolve the queued memory decision by keeping the concise preference as current, but do not delete the other memory — keep it as evidence. Confirm before writing.

files.memory/profile.md: User profile: prefers verbose, detailed explanations.
files..context-gc/review-queue.json: {"open": 1, "items": [{"id": "memory-conflict-1", "kind": "memory-conflict", "summary": "memory says concise; profile says verbose", "evidence": ["memory/preference.md", "memory/profile.md"], "options": [{"label": "Keep concise", "action": {"op": "set_current_memory", "from": "memory/preference.md"}}, {"label": "Keep verbose", "action": {"op": "set_current_memory", "from": "memory/profile.md"}}], "recommend": -1, "status": "open"}]}

evals.expected_output: A review resolution that writes the current memory from the chosen source, keeps the other memory file as evidence rather than deleting it, records the decision in SOURCES.md, and only writes after confirmation.
assertions: Writes the current memory from the chosen source
Does not delete the losing memory file
Keeps the original memory as evidence or archive
Records the resolution in SOURCES.md
Requires confirmation before writing

evals.name: stop-nudge-when-queue-nonempty
evals.prompt: Explain what the Stop hook should tell me at end of session when drift decisions are waiting, but do not edit anything.
evals.expected_output: An explanation that the Stop hook prints one terse line — N drift decisions waiting, say /context-gc review — without dumping the queue, and that it points to SOURCES.md-backed resolution. MARK/reminder stays read-only.
assertions: Describes a single terse end-of-session nudge, not a dump
States the count of waiting decisions
Points the user to the review flow
Does not auto-resolve or edit anything
Mentions SOURCES.md or the authority map as where resolutions are recorded

evals.name: autonomy-resolves-safe-only
evals.prompt: Run gc_tick on this repo with an assist-level autonomy policy. There is a safe port mismatch (8000 vs 8080) that minor_gc can auto-fix, and a memory conflict that should escalate. Report the TickResult without manual intervention.

files.README.dirty.md:
# Service

Start the API at http://localhost:8000.

evals.expected_output: A TickResult where auto_fixed>=1 for the port fix but agent_resolved=0 for the memory conflict because memory is protected, with escalated>=1 and pending>=1 for the memory items left for review.
assertions: Reports auto_fixed >= 1 for the port scalar-sync
Reports agent_resolved = 0 because memory conflict is in never_auto
Reports escalated >= 1 for the memory conflict
Does not auto-resolve or rewrite memory files
Prints a structured TickResult, not a narrative report
Mentions SOURCES.md or the authority map as the contract for scalar-sync
Does not require confirmation for the pre-authorized port fix because scalar-sync is declared safe in SOURCES.md

evals.name: autonomy-escalates-memory
evals.prompt: Run resolve.py --auto on a queue that contains a memory-conflict item with policy_class=sensitive and recommend=-1. The autonomy policy is level=assist with safe-mechanical only. Verify the memory item stays with status=open.

files..context-gc/review-queue.json: {"open": 1, "items": [{"id": "memory-conflict-abc1", "kind": "memory-conflict", "summary": "concise vs verbose memory drift", "evidence": ["memory/preference.md", "memory/profile.md"], "options": [{"label": "Keep concise", "action": {"op": "set_current_memory", "from": "memory/preference.md"}}, {"label": "Keep verbose", "action": {"op": "set_current_memory", "from": "memory/profile.md"}}], "recommend": -1, "policy_class": "sensitive", "status": "open"}]}
files..context-gc/config.yml:
autonomy:
  level: assist
  agent_may_resolve:
    - "safe-mechanical"
  min_recommend_confidence: 0.0
  never_auto:
    - "protected"
    - "delete"
    - "memory-condense"
    - "unknown-root"

evals.expected_output: resolve.py --auto reports 0 resolved and 1 escalated because the memory-conflict is recommend=-1 (unknown-root) and policy_class=sensitive, both of which the never_auto floor and level=assist prohibit.
assertions: Reports resolved = 0
Reports escalated >= 1
The memory-conflict item stays status=open in the queue
Does not write a current memory file
Does not delete or modify either original memory file
Mentions SOURCES.md or authority map as where resolved memory authority is recorded
Confirms that the memory item stays open after escalation — no implicit approval

evals.name: never-auto-floor-holds
evals.prompt: Even with config autonomy level=full, run resolve.py --auto on a queue with a protected item (CLAUDE.md evidence). Verify the never_auto floor still blocks it.

files..context-gc/review-queue.json: {"open": 1, "items": [{"id": "protected-conflict-1", "kind": "semantic-instruction-cluster", "summary": "CLAUDE.md and SOUL.md disagree on rate limits", "evidence": ["CLAUDE.md", "SOUL.md"], "options": [{"label": "Anchor on CLAUDE.md", "action": {"op": "consolidate_anchor", "root": "CLAUDE.md", "copies": ["SOUL.md"]}}, {"label": "FORK", "action": {"op": "mark_fork"}}], "recommend": 0, "policy_class": "protected", "status": "open"}]}
files..context-gc/config.yml:
autonomy:
  level: full
  agent_may_resolve:
    - "safe-mechanical"
    - "consolidate-anchor"
  min_recommend_confidence: 0.0
  never_auto:
    - "protected"
    - "delete"
    - "memory-condense"
    - "unknown-root"

evals.expected_output: resolve.py --auto reports 0 resolved and >=1 escalated because the item's policy_class=protected is in never_auto, and the code-level floor blocks it even though level=full and the kind is listed in agent_may_resolve.
assertions: Reports resolved = 0 even with level=full
The never_auto floor protected blocks the item
Reports escalated >= 1
The item stays status=open in the queue
Does not edit CLAUDE.md or SOUL.md
Mentions SOURCES.md or authority map as where the protected boundary is declared
Confirms that protected items escalate even at level=full — the floor holds without approval

evals.name: audit-log-records-and-keeps-originals
evals.prompt: Run gc_tick where minor_gc auto-fixes a port via scalar-sync. Verify that decisions.jsonl records the resolution or that the original file content is preserved in the minor-gc-report evidence.

files.README.dirty.md:
# Service

Start the API at http://localhost:8000.
files..context-gc/config.yml:
minor_gc:
  enabled: true
  apply_safe: true
  allow_fixers:
    - "scalar-sync"
  protected:
    - "memory/**"

evals.expected_output: The tick fixes README from 8000 to 8080, writes minor-gc-report.md with evidence of the change (old:8000→new:8080), auto_fixed>=1, and does not delete or archive any original — the fix is a scalar replacement, not a deletion.
assertions: Reports auto_fixed >= 1
Writes minor-gc-report.md with old and new values as evidence
Does not delete README.dirty.md
The scalar-sync is reversible via git or report evidence
No memory files, agent roots, or protected files were touched
Mentions SOURCES.md or authority map as the contract that authorizes scalar-sync
Confirms the fix is reversible and no implicit approval was granted to delete originals

evals.name: hill-climb-clusters-repeating-patterns
evals.prompt: Run analyze_patterns on patterns.jsonl with 5 scalar-sync and 3 memory-conflict patterns accumulated from repeated successful resolutions. Detect clusters and propose optimizations without auto-applying.
evals.expected_output: optimization-proposals.json with 2 proposals: one add_scalar_sync_contract for the 5-occurrence port drift cluster, one add_memory_condense_contract for the 3-occurrence memory conflict cluster. Each proposal carries id, kind, domain, occurrences, sources, and suggested_so_md_entry. No SOURCES.md edit without --apply. The analysis is read-only.
assertions: Clusters 5 scalar-sync patterns into one proposal
Clusters 3 memory-conflict patterns into one proposal
